mission.title: vllm: Support "required" option in tool_choice
mission.description: Add support for the "required" option in the tool_choice parameter of the OpenAI-compatible chat completions API. When tool_choice is set to "required", the model must call at least one tool in its response. This is critical for agent-based workflows. Requested by 25+ users.

steps.title: Check current vLLM setup
steps.description:
Verify your vLLM version and configuration:
```bash
pip show vllm | grep Version
# or
python -c "import vllm; print(vllm.__version__)"
```
The `tool_choice: "required"` option requires a recent version of vLLM with tool calling support.

steps.title: Understand tool_choice options
steps.description:
The OpenAI API supports several `tool_choice` values:
- `"auto"` - The model decides whether to call a tool (already supported)
- `"none"` - The model will not call any tool (already supported)
- `"required"` - The model must call at least one tool (this feature)
- `{"type": "function", "function": {"name": "..."}}` - Force a specific tool

The `"required"` option is essential for agentic workflows where every response must include a tool call.

steps.title: Start vLLM with tool calling enabled
steps.description:
Launch the vLLM server with a model that supports tool calling:
```bash
python -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server \
  --model <model-with-tool-support> \
  --enable-auto-tool-choice \
  --tool-call-parser hermes
```
The `--enable-auto-tool-choice` and `--tool-call-parser` flags configure the tool calling behavior.

steps.title: Verify tool_choice required works
steps.description:
Test using `tool_choice: "required"` in a chat completion request:
```bash
curl http://localhost:8000/v1/chat/completions \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "model": "<model-name>",
    "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "What is the weather in Paris?"}],
    "tools": [{"type": "function", "function": {"name": "get_weather", "parameters": {"type": "object", "properties": {"location": {"type": "string"}}}}}],
    "tool_choice": "required"
  }'
```
The response should always include a tool call to `get_weather`, regardless of the query content.

resolution.summary: vLLM added support for the "required" option in the tool_choice parameter. When set to "required", the model is constrained to always include at least one tool call in its response. This is critical for agentic AI workflows where tool calls are mandatory for task execution.
codeSnippets:
# Start vLLM with tool calling
python -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server \
  --model <model> \
  --enable-auto-tool-choice \
  --tool-call-parser hermes

# Use tool_choice: required
curl http://localhost:8000/v1/chat/completions \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"model": "<model>", "messages": [...], "tools": [...], "tool_choice": "required"}'

prerequisites.description: Python 3.8+, pip, a CUDA-capable GPU, and a working vLLM installation (pip install vllm) with a model that supports tool calling.
